channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Spanish PM visits site of deadly wildfire
item.description: Spain’s Prime Minister, Pedro Sanchez met with firefighters and emergency crews who battled a wildfire in Almeria.

item.title: US judge voids Trump’s IRS settlement, alleges self-dealing
item.description: Judge rules that US president, Department of Justice misused courts in settlement that led to 'anti-weaponization' fund.

item.title: UK designates IRGC as terrorist organisation
item.description: The UK government has designated Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation under new powers.

item.title: Locked exits, burning decor: inside Bangkok’s deadly pub fire
item.description: Survivors recount the horror after a deadly blaze engulfed a popular pub on a busy street in Bangkok.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Five biggest controversies of the 2026 tournament
item.description: Football's 2026 World Cup has been full of controversies - Al Jazeera breaks down the five most significant.

item.title: Trump administration vows to ‘disable’ International Criminal Court
item.description: US State Department ups pressure campaign on international tribunal, threatening allies who support investigations.

item.title: UK to list Iran’s IRGC as ‘terror’ threat
item.description: London debuts new powers targeting state proxies after accusing Iranian outfit of engineering anti-Semitic attacks.

item.title: How Lyhanna’s murder case is shedding light on child abuse in France
item.description: The rape and murder of 11-year-old Lyhanna exposed years of alleged police and judicial failures in France.

item.title: Staff at DR Congo Ebola centre strike as virus continues spreading
item.description: Walkout over late payments comes as public health officials confirm that the virus has reached two more provinces.

item.title: Bangkok pub fire kills 27, wounds dozens: What we know
item.description: The blaze – one of Thailand’s worst in recent history – also injured more than 70 people, many of them critically.

item.title: EU unveils $1bn fund for Gaza war recovery
item.description: The scheme falls critically short of the $71bn reportedly needed for Gaza's recovery over the next 10 years.

item.title: Person killed in shooting involving ICE agents in US state of Maine
item.description: State lawmaker says one person was killed by immigration agents in Biddeford, days after another ICE shooting in Texas.

item.title: US imposes new sanctions on Cuba tourism ministry, state-owned companies
item.description: The US Treasury Department posted the new directive on its website on Monday.

item.title: Anti-terror police take over probe into death of UK politician Widdecombe
item.description: A 28-year-old man has been re-arrested on suspicion of the commission, preparation or instigation of acts of terrorism.

item.title: India thrash England in historic first women’s cricket Test match at Lord’s
item.description: England, setting record-breaking target of 457 for victory, are dismissed for 186 shortly before lunch on the final day.

item.title: Trump: US will ‘take over’ Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Donald Trump has said the US will ‘take over’ the Strait of Hormuz, accusing Iran of breaking the deal.

item.title: Iraqi PM arrives in US seeking balance between security and economy
item.description: PM Ali al-Zaidi to hold talks with Trump, with energy investment and role of pro-Iran groups likely to dominate agenda.

item.title: Iraq seizes 375kg of gold in sweeping anti-corruption crackdown
item.description: In a massive corruption crackdown, Iraqi authorities have uncovered 375kg of gold linked to a high-profile case.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Key players to watch as France play Spain in semifinal
item.description: Spain-France clash is filled with superstars like Kylian Mbappe, Ousmane Dembele and Lamine Yamal, among others.

item.title: Trump says US will become ‘guardian’ of Strait of Hormuz and collect tolls
item.description: Iran rejects any US control over strait as both sides trade attacks in major threat to negotiations to end their war.

item.title: What is the EU’s plan to cut trade with illegal Israeli settlements?
item.description: Foreign ministers' meeting comes months after the EU imposed sanctions on Israel over West Bank settlement construction.

item.title: How Sheikh Hamad revolutionised Arab media through Al Jazeera
item.description: The late Father Emir broke the regional monopoly of state-controlled broadcasting and challenged Western media hegemony.

item.title: Yemeni government forces strike Sanaa airport
item.description: Yemeni government forces strike Sanaa airport

item.title: EU sanctions nine people over alleged Russian cyber-spying campaign
item.description: EU announces sanctions on nine individuals and four entities over alleged years-long cyber-espionage campaign.

item.title: Hamas denies UN accusations over Gaza aid distribution interference
item.description: UN accuses Hamas of obstructing aid and risking workers' safety, but the group denies it and defends its actions.
